question: Will Rhoda Magbitang win Top Chef Season 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the contestant who wins Top Chef Season 23.

If Top Chef Season 23 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Top Chef Season 23 has otherwise not concluded by JUly 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". question: Polymarket surpasses Robinhood on Similar Web in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, Polymarket.com has more monthly worldwide web visits as published by similarweb.com (https://similarweb.com/#/digitalsuite/websiteanalysis/overview/website-performance/*/999/1m?webSource=Total&key=polymarket.com%2Crobinhood.com) for at least one Monthly data point in 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any monthly data point for 2026 will qualify. If not all relevant monthly data points have been published by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

A tie in the number of Monthly Website visits will not suffice.

The resolution source will be the Worldwide Monthly Website Visits data from the "Visits over time" chart comparing Polymarket.com with Robinhood, viewed by adding the listed company to the Similarweb Polymarket.com page (https://similarweb.com/#/digitalsuite/websiteanalysis/overview/website-performance/*/999/1m?webSource=Total&key=polymarket.com%2Crobinhood.com) and selecting "M" (Monthly) in the "Visits over time" chart.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: Polymarket surpasses Robinhood on Similar Web in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, Polymarket.com has more monthly worldwide web visits as published by similarweb.com (https://similarweb.com/#/digitalsuite/websiteanalysis/overview/website-performance/*/999/1m?webSource=Total&key=polymarket.com%2Crobinhood.com) for at least one Monthly data point in 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any monthly data point for 2026 will qualify. If not all relevant monthly data points have been published by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

A tie in the number of Monthly Website visits will not suffice.

The resolution source will be the Worldwide Monthly Website Visits data from the "Visits over time" chart comparing Polymarket.com with Robinhood, viewed by adding the listed company to the Similarweb Polymarket.com page (https://similarweb.com/#/digitalsuite/websiteanalysis/overview/website-performance/*/999/1m?webSource=Total&key=polymarket.com%2Crobinhood.com) and selecting "M" (Monthly) in the "Visits over time" chart.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket's explosive web traffic surge, fueled by record-breaking election betting volumes exceeding $3.7 billion in 2024, has already propelled it past Robinhood in SimilarWeb's global finance category rankings, with monthly visits topping 68 million versus Robinhood's 28 million as of late 2024. This momentum, driven by crypto-native user acquisition and platform stickiness from real-money prediction markets, underpins the 97% yes probability, as traders project sustained dominance through 2026 amid a favorable regulatory tailwind post-U.S. election clarity. However, realistic risks include post-hype traffic normalization, CFTC enforcement tightening on offshore crypto betting, Robinhood's crypto expansion countermeasures, or technical scalability failures during peak events, any of which could prevent surpassing thresholds by year-end.

question: Will Russia capture all of Rodynske by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Rodynske, Donetsk Oblast, (48.351463° N, 37.207979° E) by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Rodynske will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of Rodynske, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/inyaBPNNyVocH72e8

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

question: Another critical Cloudflare incident by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cloudflare experiences any incident classified as Critical (red) as of the time it is marked as “Resolved” by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as critical when they are resolved, during this market's above specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to Critical will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Cloudflare (for example, on cloudflarestatus.com or cloudflarestatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the likelihood of another critical Cloudflare incident hinges on the company's history of high-profile outages, including the major global disruption on June 12, 2022, and more recent partial service interruptions like the March 12, 2024, Workers platform issue affecting API access. Recent post-incident reports highlight improvements in capacity planning and bug mitigation following a July 2024 edge network slowdown, bolstering confidence in enhanced resilience. However, persistent DDoS threats and complex global infrastructure introduce ongoing risks, with traders pricing in about a 20-30% implied probability by year-end based on past base rates of roughly one severe event annually. No major scheduled maintenance is announced, but real-time monitoring of Cloudflare's status page remains key for shifts.

question: Le Portel vs. Strasbourg
description:
In the upcoming Pro A game, scheduled for February 7 at 2:00PM ET:
If the Le Portel win, the market will resolve to "Le Portel".
If the Strasbourg win, the market will resolve to "Strasbourg".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Le Portel a slim 50.5% implied edge hosting Strasbourg in Betclic Elite Round 3 action, reflecting balanced early-season form with Le Portel at 1-1 (solid home win over Chalon, road loss to Monaco) and Strasbourg 2-0 (convincing victories against Roanne and Saint-Quentin). Home-court advantage in Pro A arenas tips the scale slightly for Le Portel, whose backcourt led by Donovan Donaldson exploits fast breaks, while Strasbourg's frontcourt duo of Davion Mintz and tandem inside presence counters effectively. No major injuries reported on official updates, but a late scratch to Le Portel's Alpha Kaba or Strasbourg's key shooters could swing odds 5-10 points; monitor final lineups for momentum shifts in this evenly matched matchup.

question: Will Bitcoin dip to $50,000 by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:00 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final "Low" price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

question: Will Artemis II launch by April 30?
description:
If the Artemis II rocket successfully launches from its launch pad by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The outcome of any launch will be corroborated by examining official video provided by NASA (https://www.youtube.com/nasa), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., an explosion) after the launch will have no bearing on the outcome. Any name change of the Artemis II with another vehicle will have no bearing on the outcome of the market.

events.title: NASA Artemis II
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the Artemis II mission.

eventMetadata.context_description: NASA's Artemis II, the first crewed Orion mission orbiting the Moon, sees trader sentiment anchored by repeated delays from technical hurdles, with the latest official target shifting to no earlier than September 2025 per NASA's October 2024 update. Key challenges include Orion heat shield ablation analysis from Artemis I, battery recharge anomalies, and Axiom Space's EVAsuit certification lags, eroding confidence in 2025 launch odds. While SLS core stage testing nears completion, integration risks persist amid SpaceX Starship competition for Artemis III. Watch Q1 2025 milestones like Orion vibration tests and the February Human Exploration Conference for catalysts that could swing market-implied probabilities, given NASA's history of 1-2 year slips.

question: MagicBlock FDV above $10M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of MagicBlock's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If MagicBlock (https://x.com/magicblock) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Backpack FDV above $500M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Backpack's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Backpack (https://x.com/Backpack) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Liftoff Mobile's market cap be less than $4.25B at market close on IPO day?
description:
This market will resolve based on Liftoff Mobile’s market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

As of market creation, the IPO is scheduled to price on February 6, 2026 (ET). If no such IPO occurs by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before April 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the specified company’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Liftoff Mobile IPO before April 2026 at 98.2% implied probability, driven by the absence of any S-1 filing, official announcements, or banker hires signaling public market preparations from the mobile app growth platform, formerly Vungle-owned and now under Blackstone. Broader headwinds in adtech and mobile gaming—intensified by Apple's ATT privacy changes curbing attribution and a dormant IPO window amid high interest rates—reinforce this positioning, with few peers like AppLovin trading at premium multiples. Realistic challenges include Federal Reserve rate cuts sparking IPO revival, explosive revenue beats in Q4 earnings, or strategic shifts like spin-offs, though these remain low-odds catalysts given current silence.

question: Bitmine sells any Ethereum in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bitmine sells any of its Ethereum by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no Clear Street Group IPO before April 2026 (98.4% implied probability), anchored by the fintech prime brokerage's recent $165 million Series B in late 2023 at a $1.75 billion valuation, providing ample runway without public market pressures. Broader market dynamics reinforce this: elevated interest rates and equity volatility have stalled fintech debuts, with peers like Stripe and Chime delaying plans amid regulatory scrutiny on digital asset services. Clear Street's focus on tech-driven capital markets for emerging managers lacks the hype for a near-term pop. Challenges include an abrupt S-1 filing—signaling confidential prep—or a macro thaw sparking a 2025 IPO window, potentially lifting valuations toward the 9-12B buckets if growth accelerates.

question: Will the ECB announce a 50+ bps decrease at the April 2026 meeting?
description:
This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the deposit facility rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the European Central Bank's (ECB) April 2026 meeting.

If the deposit facility rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 basis points and will resolve to the relevant bracket. For example, if the deposit facility rate is increased or decreased by 12.5 basis points, it will be treated as a 25 basis point change for the purposes of resolution.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the European Central Bank after its April 30, 2026 monetary policy meeting, as listed on the official ECB calendar:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/mgcgc/html/index.en.html

The level and change of the deposit facility rate is also published at the official ECB interest rates page:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html

This market may resolve as soon as the ECB releases its interest rate decision following the April 30, 2026, meeting.

If no interest rate decision or update is published by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the “No change” bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors an ECB interest rate increase in April 2026 at 51.5%, with no change nearly even at 46.7%, driven by persistent Eurozone inflation above the 2% target, sticky services prices, and robust wage growth signaling potential upside risks. Recent ECB cuts to 3.25% in October 2024 have eased policy, but cooling headline CPI and softening GDP growth temper hawkish bets, keeping the race tight amid data-dependent forward guidance from President Lagarde. Separation could emerge from Q1 2026 inflation prints, December 2025 ECB projections, or divergent US Fed paths influencing euro strength and imported inflation.

If the deposit facility rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 basis points and will resolve to the relevant bracket. For example, if the deposit facility rate is increased or decreased by 12.5 basis points, it will be treated as a 25 basis point change for the purposes of resolution.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the European Central Bank after its April 30, 2026 monetary policy meeting, as listed on the official ECB calendar:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/mgcgc/html/index.en.html

The level and change of the deposit facility rate is also published at the official ECB interest rates page:
https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html

This market may resolve as soon as the ECB releases its interest rate decision following the April 30, 2026, meeting.

If no interest rate decision or update is published by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the “No change” bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors an ECB interest rate increase in April 2026 at 51.5%, with no change nearly even at 46.7%, driven by persistent Eurozone inflation above the 2% target, sticky services prices, and robust wage growth signaling potential upside risks. Recent ECB cuts to 3.25% in October 2024 have eased policy, but cooling headline CPI and softening GDP growth temper hawkish bets, keeping the race tight amid data-dependent forward guidance from President Lagarde. Separation could emerge from Q1 2026 inflation prints, December 2025 ECB projections, or divergent US Fed paths influencing euro strength and imported inflation.

question: Spread: AD Ceuta FC (-1.5)
description:
This market will resolve to "AD Ceuta FC" if AD Ceuta FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Córdoba CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: AD Ceuta FC vs. Córdoba CF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for February 8 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Córdoba CF holds a clear edge as the higher-division side from Segunda División, driving trader consensus toward them in this Copa del Rey matchup against third-tier AD Ceuta FC, with implied probabilities reflecting about 65-70% favoritism based on recent pricing. Ceuta benefits from home-field advantage at Alfonso Murube Stadium and a resilient defensive record in recent Primera Federación fixtures, but Córdoba's attacking momentum—three wins in their last five, including a 2-0 shutout—bolsters sentiment. No major injuries reported on either side per official updates, though Córdoba's depth could exploit Ceuta's fatigue from a midweek league draw. Watch for weather in Ceuta and Córdoba's rotation strategy under coach Iván Ania, which could narrow the gap if key starters rest.

question: Will Beyoncé wear Balmain & Olivier Rousteing at the Met Gala?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Beyoncé wears pieces of clothing, jewelry, or accessories from the listed designer at any point during her appearance at the 2026 Met Gala, currently scheduled for May 4, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Beyoncé does not attend the Met Gala, this market will resolve to "No". This market may resolve only after the Met Gala has concluded. No afterparties or later events will qualify; only appearances at the Met Gala itself will count. If the Met Gala does not take place by May 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be photos or videos of the event, or statements from Beyoncé or her legal or social media representatives.

question: Another critical Cloudflare incident by May 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cloudflare experiences any incident classified as Critical (red) as of the time it is marked as “Resolved” by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as critical when they are resolved, during this market's above specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to Critical will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Cloudflare (for example, on cloudflarestatus.com or cloudflarestatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Another critical Cloudflare incident by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cloudflare experiences any incident classified as Critical (red) as of the time it is marked as “Resolved” by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as critical when they are resolved, during this market's above specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to Critical will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Cloudflare (for example, on cloudflarestatus.com or cloudflarestatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the likelihood of another critical Cloudflare incident hinges on the company's history of high-profile outages, including the major global disruption on June 12, 2022, and more recent partial service interruptions like the March 12, 2024, Workers platform issue affecting API access. Recent post-incident reports highlight improvements in capacity planning and bug mitigation following a July 2024 edge network slowdown, bolstering confidence in enhanced resilience. However, persistent DDoS threats and complex global infrastructure introduce ongoing risks, with traders pricing in about a 20-30% implied probability by year-end based on past base rates of roughly one severe event annually. No major scheduled maintenance is announced, but real-time monitoring of Cloudflare's status page remains key for shifts.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as critical when they are resolved, during this market's above specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to Critical will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Cloudflare (for example, on cloudflarestatus.com or cloudflarestatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Another critical Cloudflare incident by...?
events.description:

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as critical when they are resolved, during this market's above specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to Critical will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Cloudflare (for example, on cloudflarestatus.com or cloudflarestatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the likelihood of another critical Cloudflare incident hinges on the company's history of high-profile outages, including the major global disruption on June 12, 2022, and more recent partial service interruptions like the March 12, 2024, Workers platform issue affecting API access. Recent post-incident reports highlight improvements in capacity planning and bug mitigation following a July 2024 edge network slowdown, bolstering confidence in enhanced resilience. However, persistent DDoS threats and complex global infrastructure introduce ongoing risks, with traders pricing in about a 20-30% implied probability by year-end based on past base rates of roughly one severe event annually. No major scheduled maintenance is announced, but real-time monitoring of Cloudflare's status page remains key for shifts.

question: Will USD/JPY hit 200 (High) in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any USD/JPY hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized USD/JPY hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the USD/JPY Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-jpy-chart).

Current spot at 143.80 underscores yen strength post-BOJ's December policy hold at 0.25%, reversing carry trade flows from 2024's 161 peak. Key risks include persistent US inflation delaying Fed easing or Japan's fiscal strains capping BOJ aggression; watch January 29 BOJ meeting and February 11 FOMC for pivot signals. Market-implied probabilities factor in subdued US growth forecasts at 2.1% versus Japan's 1%, tilting toward rangebound trading around 140-150 absent shocks.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized USD/JPY hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the USD/JPY Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-jpy-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects subdued odds for USD/JPY hitting elevated levels in 2026, driven primarily by narrowing US-Japan interest rate differentials as the Fed executes further cuts—projected to 3.75-4% by year-end—while the BOJ continues gradual hikes toward 1%. Current spot at 143.80 underscores yen strength post-BOJ's December policy hold at 0.25%, reversing carry trade flows from 2024's 161 peak. Key risks include persistent US inflation delaying Fed easing or Japan's fiscal strains capping BOJ aggression; watch January 29 BOJ meeting and February 11 FOMC for pivot signals. Market-implied probabilities factor in subdued US growth forecasts at 2.1% versus Japan's 1%, tilting toward rangebound trading around 140-150 absent shocks.

question: Karoline Leavitt out by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Karoline Leavitt ceases to be the White House Press Secretary for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Leavitt's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect. Leaves of absence, such as maternity leave, will qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Karoline Leavitt's recent appointment as White House Press Secretary following Donald Trump's January 2025 inauguration drives the 98.3% implied probability on "No" for her departure by March 31, reflecting trader consensus in her stability amid the administration's early phase. As a longtime Trump campaign spokesperson with no reported gaffes, internal conflicts, or performance issues in initial briefings, markets price in minimal risk of abrupt exit. This high confidence aligns with historical patterns where new press secretaries in honeymoon periods endure absent scandals. Realistic shifts could stem from a major briefing controversy, White House staffing realignments, or unforeseen personal developments, though such catalysts remain absent.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a modest implied probability for GBP/USD reaching the target level in 2026, primarily driven by persistent UK-US interest rate differentials favoring the dollar amid the Federal Reserve's projected 100bps easing cycle through 2025 versus the Bank of England's cautious 25bps trim. Spot GBP/USD trades near 1.305, down 3% YTD, reflecting sticky UK services inflation (CPI 2.3%) and softer GDP growth (0.6% Q/Q) versus robust US data. Market-implied odds hinge on BOE hawkishness post-spring budget and potential sterling rebounds from fiscal loosening; watch October 31 UK Budget and December FOMC for catalysts, with historical cable ranges (1.20-1.40) underscoring volatility risks.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized GBP/USD hourly candle low price is equal to or below the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “L” figure located at the top of the GBP/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/gbp-usd-chart).

Market-implied odds hinge on BOE hawkishness post-spring budget and potential sterling rebounds from fiscal loosening; watch October 31 UK Budget and December FOMC for catalysts, with historical cable ranges (1.20-1.40) underscoring volatility risks.

question: Will Nicole Kidman wear Tom Ford at the Met Gala?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Nicole Kidman wears pieces of clothing, jewelry, or accessories from the listed designer at any point during her appearance at the 2026 Met Gala, currently scheduled for May 4, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Nicole Kidman does not attend the Met Gala, this market will resolve to "No". This market may resolve only after the Met Gala has concluded. No afterparties or later events will qualify; only appearances at the Met Gala itself will count. If the Met Gala does not take place by May 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be photos or videos of the event, or statements from Nicole Kidman or her legal or social media representatives.

question: Will USD/KRW hit 2000 (High) in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any USD/KRW hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized USD/KRW hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the USD/KRW Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-krw-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for USD/KRW hitting targeted levels in 2026 centers on widening US-South Korea monetary policy divergence and ongoing political instability in Seoul. The pair trades around 1,380 after spiking to 1,480 in December amid President Yoon's impeachment crisis, reflecting safe-haven USD flows. Bank of Korea's 3.50% benchmark lags the Fed's 4.25-4.50% range, supporting USD strength, though expected 2025 Fed cuts may cap upside. Key drivers include SK export reliance on chips amid China tensions and potential US tariffs. Traders eye BOK's January 17 decision and February US GDP data as pivotal for long-term trajectories, with market-implied odds capturing real-money bets on Won weakness.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any USD/KRW hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized USD/KRW hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the USD/KRW Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (https://www.investing.com/currencies/usd-krw-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for USD/KRW hitting targeted levels in 2026 centers on widening US-South Korea monetary policy divergence and ongoing political instability in Seoul. The pair trades around 1,380 after spiking to 1,480 in December amid President Yoon's impeachment crisis, reflecting safe-haven USD flows. Bank of Korea's 3.50% benchmark lags the Fed's 4.25-4.50% range, supporting USD strength, though expected 2025 Fed cuts may cap upside. Key drivers include SK export reliance on chips amid China tensions and potential US tariffs. Traders eye BOK's January 17 decision and February US GDP data as pivotal for long-term trajectories, with market-implied odds capturing real-money bets on Won weakness.

question: Will Bitcoin dip to $20,000 by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:00 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final "Low" price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

events.title: What price will Bitcoin hit in 2026?
events.description: What price will Bitcoin hit before 2027?

question: Will Lucy Powell be the next Prime Minister of the United Kingdom in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed by the United Kingdom Monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Next PM in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United Kingdom; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next UK Prime Minister in 2026?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 37.5% chance of no new UK prime minister in 2026, signaling confidence that Keir Starmer will serve out his term amid the fixed parliamentary schedule targeting a 2029 election, barring unforeseen resignation or snap poll. Angela Rayner leads alternatives at 21.5% as Labour deputy prime minister and a figure bridging party factions amid recent internal revolts, including 37 MPs defying the government on welfare reforms in October 2024. Ed Miliband's 11.7% reflects his energy secretary role despite net zero policy backlash, while Nigel Farage's 8.7% odds track Reform UK's poll surge to rival Labour among working-class voters. Starmer's approval ratings have plunged to historic lows per YouGov surveys, fueling speculation but not yet shifting leadership dynamics decisively.

events.title: Next UK Prime Minister in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be officially appointed by the United Kingdom Monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Next PM in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United Kingdom; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 37.5% chance of no new UK prime minister in 2026, signaling confidence that Keir Starmer will serve out his term amid the fixed parliamentary schedule targeting a 2029 election, barring unforeseen resignation or snap poll. Angela Rayner leads alternatives at 21.5% as Labour deputy prime minister and a figure bridging party factions amid recent internal revolts, including 37 MPs defying the government on welfare reforms in October 2024. Ed Miliband's 11.7% reflects his energy secretary role despite net zero policy backlash, while Nigel Farage's 8.7% odds track Reform UK's poll surge to rival Labour among working-class voters. Starmer's approval ratings have plunged to historic lows per YouGov surveys, fueling speculation but not yet shifting leadership dynamics decisively.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Next PM in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of the United Kingdom; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 37.5% chance of no new UK prime minister in 2026, signaling confidence that Keir Starmer will serve out his term amid the fixed parliamentary schedule targeting a 2029 election, barring unforeseen resignation or snap poll. Angela Rayner leads alternatives at 21.5% as Labour deputy prime minister and a figure bridging party factions amid recent internal revolts, including 37 MPs defying the government on welfare reforms in October 2024. Ed Miliband's 11.7% reflects his energy secretary role despite net zero policy backlash, while Nigel Farage's 8.7% odds track Reform UK's poll surge to rival Labour among working-class voters. Starmer's approval ratings have plunged to historic lows per YouGov surveys, fueling speculation but not yet shifting leadership dynamics decisively.

question: Major CEX insolvent in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if official information released by any of the following major centralized exchanges: Binance, Coinbase, Bybit, OKX, or Kraken, official representatives of such exchange (e.g. lawyers, spokespeople, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting announces by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET that the exchange is insolvent or is filing/has filed for any variety of bankruptcy. 

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

question: Will Gavin McKenna be drafted 1st overall in the 2026 NHL Draft?
description:
This market will resolve according to the player drafted No. 1 overall in the 2026 NHL Draft scheduled for June 26-27, 2026. 

If the 2026 NHL Draft is postponed, this market will stay open until its completion.

If the 2026 NHL draft is cancelled or the first pick is not definitively known by July 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL, including the live broadcast of the 2026 NHL draft; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin McKenna's blistering start to the 2024-25 WHL season, leading the CHL in scoring with 10 goals and multiple hat tricks through Medicine Hat's early games, cements his 69.5% trader consensus as the 2026 NHL Draft's top pick. The 6-foot-1 winger's elite shot, speed, and playmaking echo generational talents, per latest NHL Central Scouting radars and beat reports. Ivar Stenberg's climb to 24.5% reflects his dynamic U18 Worlds and Hlinka Gretzky Cup exploits, highlighting the Swedish center's vision and puck skills amid rising international buzz. Trailing prospects like Verhoeff lag due to inconsistent junior production, underscoring early momentum's sway in long-term draft markets.

question: Will the Bank of Mexico announce a decrease at the May meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's May 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for May 7, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their May meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Mexico Decision in May
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's May 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for May 7, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their May meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors a Bank of Mexico rate decrease at 57.5% implied probability for the May 9 decision, propelled by cooling inflation—headline CPI dipped to 4.20% year-over-year in April from 4.44% prior—and Banxico's aggressive 50 basis-point cut to 11% in March amid sluggish GDP growth. These dynamics signal a disinflationary path toward the 3% target, bolstering easing expectations despite high real rates. No-change odds at 38% capture peso weakness from June election risks and sticky core inflation at 3.84%, while a hike at 18.5% remains a tail risk given softening price pressures and global rate divergence.

question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day by December 31, 2027?
description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2027".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors no OpenAI IPO by end-2027 at 31% implied probability, driven by ongoing restructuring from nonprofit roots, regulatory scrutiny on AI safety, and ample private funding amid Microsoft's dominant stake limiting public float needs. Valuation buckets cluster tightly above 500B, reflecting recent 157B tender offer premium fueled by 3.6B annualized revenue run-rate, yet tempered by persistent losses and cooling AI hype. Competitive pressures from Anthropic's 40B valuation, xAI's rapid scaling, and open-source challengers like Meta erode moat, with profitability thresholds and Fed rate trajectory pivotal for trillion-dollar outcomes.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors no OpenAI IPO by end-2027 at 31% implied probability, driven by ongoing restructuring from nonprofit roots, regulatory scrutiny on AI safety, and ample private funding amid Microsoft's dominant stake limiting public float needs. Valuation buckets cluster tightly above 500B, reflecting recent 157B tender offer premium fueled by 3.6B annualized revenue run-rate, yet tempered by persistent losses and cooling AI hype. Competitive pressures from Anthropic's 40B valuation, xAI's rapid scaling, and open-source challengers like Meta erode moat, with profitability thresholds and Fed rate trajectory pivotal for trillion-dollar outcomes.

question: Will SpaceX’s initial public offering occur in March 2026 (ET)?
description:
This market will resolve based on the calendar month (ET) in 2026 in which SpaceX’s initial public offering (IPO) occurs.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2027".

Announcements, filings, or scheduling of an IPO will not suffice; this market will resolve after public trading has begun.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the SpaceX IPO market favors June at a leading 33.5% implied probability, propelled by the company's blockbuster $210 billion private valuation from a recent insider tender offer, which underscores operational scale and liquidity signaling potential public readiness. Yet, the fragmented odds—spanning months like July (12.4%) and a 12.5% chance of no IPO before 2027—highlight deep uncertainty, differentiated by Elon Musk's firm stance against listing until Mars reusability is proven, Starlink's possible separate spin-off, and ample private capital inflows obviating near-term pressure. Traders weigh speculative momentum in summer months against these long-term strategic hurdles.
